description: Enterprise alert operations dashboard. Real-time view of firing alerts, alert history, top noisy alerts, alert fatigue metrics, MTTA/MTTR tracking, and alert routing health. Built for Grafana admins and oncall leads.

panels.description: Total currently firing alerts. Investigate any non-zero value during normal operations.
panels.title: Total Firing Alerts

panels.description: Critical severity alerts require immediate attention. Any non-zero triggers page.
panels.title: Critical Alerts

panels.description: Warning severity alerts. Should be investigated within SLA but not page-worthy.
panels.title: Warning Alerts

panels.description: Alerts in pending state (for-duration condition not yet met). High numbers indicate borderline conditions across services.
panels.title: Pending Alerts

panels.description: Number of distinct alert rules currently firing. Helps gauge whether alerts are from many rules or repeated instances of few.
panels.title: Alert Groups

panels.description: Active silences suppressing alerts. High counts may mask real issues — review regularly.
panels.title: Active Silences

panels.description: Alert count trends. Rising trends indicate growing instability. Correlation with deployments visible via annotations.
panels.title: Firing Alerts Over Time

panels.description: Firing alerts broken down by severity level over time.
panels.title: Alerts by Severity

panels.description: Alerts that fired most frequently in the selected time range. Top candidates for tuning thresholds, adding hysteresis, or converting to notifications. High-frequency alerts cause oncall fatigue.
panels.title: Top 10 Noisiest Alerts (Most Firings)

panels.description: Alerts that have been continuously firing the longest. Stale firing alerts (>24h) indicate either an unresolved issue or a poorly tuned threshold.
panels.title: Top 10 Longest Firing Alerts

panels.description: Rate of alerts received and processed. Invalid alerts indicate malformed alert payloads.
panels.title: Alerts Resolved/s

panels.description: Notification delivery rate broken down by channel type (slack, email, pagerduty, webhook, teams, etc.).
panels.title: Notification Delivery by Channel

panels.description: Failed notification deliveries. Non-zero values require checking contact point configuration and downstream service availability.
panels.title: Notification Failures by Channel

panels.description: 99th percentile notification delivery time. Values above 30s indicate slow contact points (SMTP relay, rate-limited webhooks, etc.).
panels.title: Notification Latency P99

panels.description: Notification volume per receiver/route. Helps identify which receivers are handling the most traffic.
panels.title: Dispatched Notifications by Receiver

panels.description: Detailed table of all currently firing alerts. Use this to identify which specific instances and jobs are affected.
panels.title: Currently Firing Alerts Table

panels.description: Silence lifecycle. Growing expired silences indicate cleanup is needed. Persistent active silences should be reviewed.
panels.title: Silence State Over Time

panels.description: Alerts currently suppressed by inhibition rules or silences.
panels.title: Inhibition Rules Active

panels.description: Overall alert processing throughput. Sustained high values may indicate alert storm conditions.
panels.title: Alert Throughput

title: Grafana Alerts — Overview & Management
